Package neqsim.process.chemistry

Class ChemicalInteractionRule

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.chemistry.ChemicalInteractionRule
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class ChemicalInteractionRule extends Object implements Serializable
Encodes a single rule of chemical interaction or chemical / fluid / material incompatibility.

Rules are loaded from src/main/resources/data/chemical_compatibility_rules.csv via loadDefaultRules(). Each rule has two operands and an interaction context, plus a severity classification and a recommended mitigation. Wildcards (*) match any value for an operand field.

Severity grading:

  • ChemicalInteractionRule.Severity.HIGH — incompatible, do not co-inject without bench validation.
  • ChemicalInteractionRule.Severity.MEDIUM — caution, expected performance loss or process upset.
  • ChemicalInteractionRule.Severity.LOW — possible interaction, confirm by lab test.
  • ChemicalInteractionRule.Severity.INFO — informational, no action required.
Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

  • Constructor Details

    • ChemicalInteractionRule

      public ChemicalInteractionRule(String chemical1Type, String chemical1Ingredient, String chemical2Type, String chemical2Ingredient, String condition, ChemicalInteractionRule.Severity severity, String mechanism, String mitigation)
      Constructs a rule.
      Parameters:
      chemical1Type - type of operand 1 (or wildcard *)
      chemical1Ingredient - ingredient family of operand 1 (or wildcard)
      chemical2Type - type of operand 2 (or wildcard)
      chemical2Ingredient - ingredient family of operand 2 (or wildcard)
      condition - free-text condition tag (e.g. co_injection, produced_water, operating)
      severity - severity classification
      mechanism - description of the underlying mechanism
      mitigation - recommended mitigation

  • Method Details

    • loadDefaultRules

      public static List<ChemicalInteractionRule> loadDefaultRules()
      Loads the default rule set from the bundled resource.
      Returns:
      list of rules (empty if the resource cannot be loaded)

    • loadFromResource

      public static List<ChemicalInteractionRule> loadFromResource(String resource)
      Loads rules from an arbitrary classpath resource.
      Parameters:
      resource - path to the CSV resource
      Returns:
      list of parsed rules

    • matches

      public boolean matches(ProductionChemical a, ProductionChemical b)
      Tests whether this rule matches the ordered pair of chemicals (a, b). Matching is symmetric: the rule matches if (a, b) or (b, a) satisfy the operand constraints.
      Parameters:
      a - first chemical
      b - second chemical (may be null for unary rules)
      Returns:
      true if the rule applies

    • isEnvironmentRule

      public boolean isEnvironmentRule()
      Tests whether the rule's second operand encodes a fluid / operating condition (rather than a chemical type), and the condition is satisfied. Recognised tags include water_high_* and material_*.
      Returns:
      true if operand 2 is an environment tag

    • environmentMatches

      public boolean environmentMatches(double temperatureC, double calciumMgL, double ironMgL, double bicarbonateMgL, String material)
      Returns true if this rule's environment operand matches the supplied environment fields.
      Parameters:
      temperatureC - operating temperature in Celsius
      calciumMgL - calcium concentration in mg/L
      ironMgL - iron concentration in mg/L
      bicarbonateMgL - bicarbonate concentration in mg/L
      material - material identifier (e.g. carbon_steel, 316L)
      Returns:
      true if the environment matches

    • checkThreshold

      private static boolean checkThreshold(String spec, String key, double value)
      Parses a threshold spec like "Ca>2000mgL" or "T>80C".
      Parameters:
      spec - specification string
      key - expected key prefix
      value - the actual numeric value to test
      Returns:
      true if the threshold is exceeded

    • matchOperand

      private static boolean matchOperand(String typeSpec, String ingredientSpec, ProductionChemical c)
      Tests whether a chemical matches an operand (type and ingredient with wildcard support).
      Parameters:
      typeSpec - type token from the rule
      ingredientSpec - ingredient token from the rule
      c - chemical to test
      Returns:
      true on match

      public String getChemical1Type()
      Gets the operand 1 type token.
      Returns:
